title: Story
description: A unit of work defined by outcome + executable acceptance + rebuild guidance. The authoritative contract; all story YAMLs are validated against this at parse time.

id.description: Unique positive integer. Must match the filename (stories/<id>.yml).

title.description: Short human-readable title. Label only — not part of the proof hash.

outcome.description: Plain-English value statement. Why this story exists and what it delivers. Must be expressible as one sentence without conjunctions (no 'and', 'also', 'as well as'); otherwise split the story.

status.description: Lifecycle state. Only agentic-verify may write `tested` or `healthy`; humans (and the story-writer agent) may write the others.

patterns.description: IDs of patterns (slugs) this story applies. Each must reference an existing patterns/<id>.yml file. Defaults to []. The story-writer always writes this field explicitly, even when empty.

tests.description: One or more test entries. A story has a one-to-many relationship with tests; each entry has its own justification.

file.description: Path to a test file (relative to repo root). Must exist. Must be referenced by exactly one story (1-to-1 binding). Test files live under crates/*/tests/ or scripts/verify/.

justification.description: What THIS specific test proves and why it's sufficient for its scope. Each test in the array gets its own justification — no aggregate justification.

uat.description: Prose walkthrough a UAT agent or human executes end-to-end. Must include cleanup. Free-form; serves as guidance rather than a strict script.

guidance.description: Non-obvious technical context needed to rebuild this component from scratch. Include only what an agent would NOT independently derive from outcome + acceptance alone. Length is unconstrained — split the story if it covers multiple objectives, not because guidance grew.

depends_on.description: IDs of other stories that must reach `tested` before this story can start. Cycles are rejected at load time.
